Video post. Looth Lite gated embed. Chapter timestamps as separate

anchors with class lg-ts-link — dash CSS should grey + pointer-events:none for non-members to close the gate-bypass via YouTube.

blocks.tagline: Ian sits down with Anders Nicklasson of True Temperament to trace the 20-year arc behind the squiggly frets — how the temperament was developed by ear, what luthiers can buy today, and why three-chord players hear the difference first.

blocks.html:
00:00:00 — Introduction & The 20-Year Journey to True Temperament Frets

00:02:14 — How the Temperament Was Developed & Dialed In by Ear

00:04:23 — Nut & Bridge: Straight Lines With All Compensation in the Frets

00:05:53 — What Luthiers Can Buy: Fretboards, Necks & Custom Options

00:08:02 — Serviceability: Leveling, Crowning & Replacing Frets

00:11:07 — Q&A — Playing in All Keys, String Gauge & Acoustic Guitars

00:14:17 — Cort Collaboration & How True Temperament Raises Guitar Value

00:19:53 — Steve Vai, Strandberg & How Artist Relationships Began

00:22:20 — Player Learning Curve, Bending & Playing With Other Musicians

00:25:13 — Conversions, Service Points & Peterson Tuner Integration

00:30:12 — Factory Tour & How Guitar Production Came About

00:38:18 — What Temperament Is It? Just Intonation vs Equal Temperament

00:41:46 — Historical Context: Gut Frets, Piano & Why We Forgot Just Intonation

00:47:55 — Who Benefits Most: Three-Chord Players & Distorted Sounds

00:53:00 — Can Players Hear the Difference & Closing Q&A
